evals.prompt: Create a Getting Started page for a developer API product. It should have a brief intro, then use tabs to show installation for Node.js, Python, and cURL.
evals.expected_output: A valid GitBook markdown page with a {% tabs %} block containing three named tabs, each with the appropriate installation command in a code block.
expectations: Output contains a {% tabs %} block with exactly three {% tab %} entries
Each tab has a title matching one of: Node.js, Python, cURL
Each tab contains a fenced code block with the appropriate language tag
Both {% tabs %} and {% tab %} blocks are properly closed with their {% end* %} tags

evals.prompt: Add a warning hint to a page explaining that API keys must never be committed to source control, and a tip hint below it pointing users to environment variable best practices.
evals.expected_output: Two GitBook hint blocks: one with style="warning" about API key security, and one with style="tip" about environment variables.
expectations: The warning hint mentions API keys and source control
Both hint blocks are closed with {% endhint %}

evals.prompt: Generate a SUMMARY.md for a docs site with three top-level sections: Getting Started (with pages: Quickstart, Installation, Authentication), API Reference (with pages: Overview, Endpoints, Errors), and Changelog.
evals.expected_output: A valid GitBook SUMMARY.md with the correct nested list structure, relative links, and section groupings.
expectations: File contains exactly three top-level sections
Getting Started section has exactly three child pages
API Reference section has exactly three child pages
All links use relative paths ending in .md
